title: None dereference before guard
description: display_name now reads user.display_name before checking whether user is None.

expected_issues.label: none-dereference
expected_issues.description: user.display_name is accessed before the None guard, so display_name(None) raises AttributeError.

manual_review_notes: A correct review should flag the attribute access before the None check. Different wording is acceptable.

title: SQL injection through f-string query
description: Search now interpolates a user-controlled term into SQL.

files.base:
def search(conn, term):
    return conn.execute("SELECT * FROM docs WHERE title LIKE ?", [f"%{term}%"])

expected_issues.label: sql-injection
expected_issues.description: term is interpolated into SQL instead of passed as a parameter.

manual_review_notes: A correct review should mention user-controlled SQL interpolation or missing parameterization.

title: Authorization token logging
description: Webhook failure logging now includes the Authorization header value.

files.base:
def handle(headers, logger):
    logger.error("webhook failed")

expected_issues.label: secret-logging
expected_issues.description: Authorization token can be written to logs.

manual_review_notes: A correct review should flag sensitive header/token logging.

title: Mixed unsafe YAML and N+1 owner lookup
description: A single file introduces unsafe YAML loading and an N+1 owner lookup. The parameterized audit query is an intentional non-issue.

expected_issues.label: unsafe-yaml-load
expected_issues.description: yaml.load is used for configuration text instead of yaml.safe_load.

expected_issues.label: n-plus-one
expected_issues.description: dashboard now calls repo.get_owner once per project instead of batching owners.

manual_review_notes: A correct review should catch both YAML and N+1. It should not report SQL injection for the parameterized audit query.

title: Parameterized SQL clean case
description: Search keeps using parameterized SQL while refactoring query construction.

files.base:
def search(conn, term):
    return conn.execute("SELECT * FROM docs WHERE title LIKE ?", [f"%{term}%"])

manual_review_notes: A security finding for SQL injection here should be judged as a false positive because the query remains parameterized.

title: None guard clean case
description: display_name is refactored but still checks user is None before accessing attributes.
manual_review_notes: A None dereference finding here should be judged as a false positive because the guard still precedes attribute access.

title: Existing related tests for profile fallback
description: The source changes display-name fallback behavior, and an existing related test file is present in repository context.
manual_review_notes: A broad claim that no related tests exist should be judged as false. A narrow claim about missing fallback-to-email coverage may be reasonable but should be noted separately.

title: Benchmark-dependent cache behavior
description: A cache flag is disabled. Whether this is harmful depends on workload size and benchmark data not present in the diff.

files.base:
def render_items(renderer, items):
    return renderer.render(items, use_cache=True)
files.head:
def render_items(renderer, items):
    return renderer.render(items, use_cache=False)

expected_issues.label: benchmark-dependent-performance
expected_issues.description: Disabling cache may hurt performance, but confirming impact requires benchmark/workload evidence.

known_hard_to_detect: Static review cannot prove whether disabling this cache is a regression without workload and benchmark evidence.
manual_review_notes: If the model reports this as a hard performance bug without evidence, judge it as overconfident. A cautious warning is acceptable.

title: Python syntax blocker
description: A missing closing brace makes the module fail to import, so the application cannot run this code path at all.

files.base:
def parse_record(raw):
    return {"id": raw["id"], "name": raw["name"]}

expected_issues.label: python-syntax-error
expected_issues.description: The dict literal is missing a closing brace, so importing this module raises SyntaxError.

manual_review_notes: A correct review should flag that the changed file cannot parse/import, even if it calls it SyntaxError, syntax issue, or build blocker.

title: Python conditional divide-by-zero crash
description: A guard for total == 0 is removed, so completion_ratio crashes only for the zero-total input context.

files.head:
def completion_ratio(done, total):
    return done / total

expected_issues.label: zero-division
expected_issues.description: Calling completion_ratio(done, 0) now raises ZeroDivisionError instead of returning 0.0.

manual_review_notes: A correct review should mention the removed zero guard or division-by-zero for total == 0.

title: Python authorization logic inverted
description: A permission check still runs but returns the opposite result for document owners, creating a logic bug without a crash.

expected_issues.label: authorization-logic-inverted
expected_issues.description: Non-admin users can delete documents they do not own, while owners are denied, because == was changed to !=.

manual_review_notes: A correct review should identify the inverted ownership check. It may classify this as bug or security; either is acceptable in manual judgement.

title: Python counter lock removed
description: A thread-safe counter update is changed to an unlocked read-modify-write sequence, creating a race under concurrent calls.

expected_issues.label: thread-race
expected_issues.description: increment no longer holds the lock while doing a read-modify-write, so concurrent calls can lose updates.

known_hard_to_detect: Static review can identify the removed lock, but cannot prove the schedule without a concurrent test.
manual_review_notes: A correct review should mention race, lost update, or lock removal. It should not require runtime proof in static mode.

title: Python file handle leak
description: A context manager is replaced with a manual open call and no close, leaking the file descriptor on success and exception paths.

expected_issues.label: resource-leak
expected_issues.description: The opened file handle is never closed because the with statement was removed.

manual_review_notes: A correct review should flag missing close/context manager or file descriptor leak.

title: Python clean locked counter
description: The counter is refactored while keeping the lock around mutation and return, so it should not produce race or resource leak findings.
manual_review_notes: A race or missing-lock finding here should be judged as a false positive because the mutation still happens inside the lock.

title: C++ compile blocker missing semicolon
description: A missing semicolon after a local variable declaration makes this C++ file fail to compile.

expected_issues.label: cpp-compile-error
expected_issues.description: The declaration `int total = a + b` is missing a semicolon, so the file does not compile.

manual_review_notes: A correct review should identify the missing semicolon or compilation failure.

title: C++ null pointer dereference before guard
description: The code reads user->name before checking whether user is nullptr, so missing users crash only in that input context.

expected_issues.label: cpp-null-dereference
expected_issues.description: `user->name` is evaluated before the nullptr guard, so displayName(nullptr) dereferences a null pointer.

manual_review_notes: A correct review should flag the dereference before the nullptr check.

title: C++ dangling c_str pointer
description: The function now returns a pointer into a local std::string that is destroyed at function exit.

expected_issues.label: dangling-pointer
expected_issues.description: Returning label.c_str() returns a pointer to storage owned by a local string that is destroyed on return.

manual_review_notes: A correct review should mention dangling pointer, use-after-free, invalid lifetime, or returning c_str from a local string.

title: C++ early return memory leak
description: A new early return skips delete after allocating a Widget with new, leaking memory on the disabled path.

expected_issues.label: cpp-memory-leak
expected_issues.description: When enabled is false, the function returns nullptr without deleting the allocated Widget.

manual_review_notes: A correct review should flag the missing delete on the early return path or recommend RAII/unique_ptr.

title: C++ cache mutex removed
description: A shared cache mutation no longer takes the mutex, so concurrent put calls can race.

expected_issues.label: cpp-data-race
expected_issues.description: The shared data_ map is mutated without holding mu_, creating a data race under concurrent access.

known_hard_to_detect: Static review can identify the removed lock, but cannot prove a concrete interleaving without a thread test or sanitizer run.
manual_review_notes: A correct review should mention removed mutex, data race, or unsynchronized shared map mutation.

title: C++ clean RAII ownership
description: The code keeps std::unique_ptr ownership and adds a simple field assignment, so it should not produce memory leak or dangling pointer findings.
manual_review_notes: A memory leak, dangling pointer, or raw ownership finding here should be judged as a false positive because ownership remains RAII-based.
